title: Vices and Virtues
summary: The repository artifact is the pinned CME serialization of University of Michigan item VicesV1. U-M keyed a selection from the exact 1967 Oxford University Press reprint identified in its source call number: three reprint title pages and Holthausen's Middle English text on odd printed pages 3-151, but not the facing modern English translation on even pages 2-150. The 1967 issue explicitly reprints Ferdinand Holthausen's 1888 EETS Original Series 89 Part I, whose continuous Middle English text is identified by EETS as edited from the unique, acephalous witness now called British Library Stowe MS 34 and formerly called Stowe MS 240. Holthausen's 1921 Part II supplies notes and glossary but is not an ancestor of the U-M text. Judith Crawford's 1987 thesis is a later direct manuscript re-edition and includes only two selected manuscript photographs, not a complete facsimile. A complete public facsimile of Stowe MS 34 was not found in the current BL catalog, official digitised-manuscript list, or endpoint audit as of 2026-07-15; BL offers onsite consultation and fee-based photography. The complete 1888 Part I, complete 1921 Part II, complete Crawford thesis, official 2026 EETS list, and U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A have been cached locally as research evidence.
review_notes: The 1967 OUP reprint is modeled as a distinct carrier/edition entity. Its version_of relation to 1888 is supported by its explicit reprint statement and matching body pagination, not inferred merely from a later catalog record.

The U-M selection boundary is explicit: the Middle English odd pages are encoded and the facing modern English translation is omitted. Translation language and rights do not silently transfer into the repository work representation.
Current Stowe MS 34 and former Stowe MS 240 designate this witness. Current Stowe MS 240 is an unrelated nineteenth-century transcript volume and must never be substituted for it.
The British Library's current 49-folio extent, Crawford's 48 surviving leaves, and the f. 38r/f. 39 hand-boundary descriptions remain unresolved parallel assertions.

Access and rights statements are entity- and component-specific. Public-domain medieval or nineteenth-century verbal content does not automatically settle rights or provider terms for U-M markup, BSB/Google scans, Crawford's thesis, Internet Archive derivatives, or future BL photography. The CME root Unlicense is recorded for contributor-owned software and repository contributions, but its software-worded grant is not treated as blanket permission for inherited U-M XML, data, or markup or as overriding the embedded 1997 terms.
Negative public-facsimile findings are date-bounded and do not prove that no unindexed, internal, private, or legacy photographic surrogate exists.

entities.label: Vices and Virtues as an abstract anonymous work
entities.description: The anonymous Middle English prose dialogue as an intellectual work, known only in an acephalous state from Stowe MS 34. This abstract boundary separates the medieval composition from its physical witness, scholarly editions, translations, digital encodings, and repository artifact.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for Vices+V1
entities.description: The exact pinned XML artifact consumed by General Corpus and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 208,013 bytes at the reviewed CME commit.

The pinned CME root LICENSE is the Unlicense and repeatedly describes its subject as software. No path-specific statement was found expressly applying that grant to the inherited U-M XML, data, or markup, so its scope for this artifact remains ambiguous.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item VicesV1
alternate_labels: Vices and Virtues: Part I Text and Translation
entities.description: A 1997 keyed and structurally marked-up selection from the 1967 OUP reprint of Holthausen's Part I. It includes three title pages and the Middle English text on odd printed pages 3-151, but omits the facing modern English translation on even pages 2-150.
notes: The source header's title says Text and Translation, but the encoded BODY contains only the Middle English odd pages.
Sencor keyboarded the text; the header reports five-percent proofing to an error rate of 1 in 20,000, Janet Erickson's markup, and Jason Chu's proof and markup review.

entities.label: OUP 1967 reprint of Vices and Virtues, Part I
entities.description: The exact physical print source named by the U-M header and call number. Its transcribed preliminary pages say First Published 1888 and Reprinted 1967, identify EETS Original Series 89, and carry a London/New York/Toronto Oxford University Press imprint.

bibliographic.citation: Ferdinand Holthausen, ed. and trans., Vices and Virtues: Being a Soul's Confession of its Sins with Reason's Description of the Virtues, Part I: Text and Translation, EETS Original Series 89 (London, New York, and Toronto: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society, 1967 reprint of the 1888 edition).
bibliographic.title: Vices and Virtues: Part I: Text and Translation
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 89
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: The 1967 title pages and carrier are distinct from the 1888 issue even where the body text and pagination are retained.
The ISBN now used for the backlist listing is attached only to the separately modeled current backlist carrier, not retroactively asserted as an original 1967 identifier.

entities.label: Ferdinand Holthausen, Vices and Virtues, Part I (1888)
entities.description: The first printed edition, with Middle English text on odd pages 3-151 and Holthausen's facing modern English translation on even pages 2-150.

bibliographic.citation: Ferdinand Holthausen, ed. and trans., Vices and Virtues: Being a Soul's Confession of its Sins with Reason's Description of the Virtues: A Middle-English Dialogue of About 1200 A.D., Part I: Text and Translation, EETS Original Series 89 (London: N. Trübner and Co., 1888).
bibliographic.title: Vices and Virtues: Part I: Text and Translation
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 89

notes: The inspected original title page says 1888. The current EETS 2026 list says 1887; that isolated conflicting year is not propagated.
Part I is text and translation. Notes and glossary were published separately as Part II in 1921.

entities.label: British Library Stowe MS 34
entities.description: The unique acephalous manuscript witness of Vices and Virtues, written on vellum in the early thirteenth century. The work occupies surviving fols. 1r-48r; its missing first eight-leaf quire contained the original opening. The current BL catalog describes 49 folios, while Crawford describes 48 surviving leaves in six gatherings of eight.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.notes: Gunn describes the manuscript more narrowly as first quarter of the thirteenth century.

holding.notes: The former shelfmark is historically secure but collides with an unrelated current Stowe MS 240. Always request and cite current Stowe MS 34.

physical_description.extent: Current BL catalog: 49 folios; Crawford: 48 surviving leaves in six gatherings of eight
physical_description.format: Small quarto in eighteenth-century crimson morocco
physical_description.dimensions: Approximately 22 x 16 cm according to Crawford
notes: The beginning is wanting; Crawford reconstructs the lost first quire as eight leaves.
A strong change of hand is placed at f. 39 by the BL catalog and at f. 38r by Crawford, who says the second scribe fills f. 37v.
William Fleetwood's autograph appears on the last leaf, and Thomas Astle's name appears at the beginning.
The work locus is ff. 1r-48r; Holthausen and U-M additionally map this as manuscript pages 1-95.
The BL Digitised Content field is blank and no entry for Stowe MS 34 appears in the current official digitised-manuscript list.

entities.label: BSB/Google complete digital facsimile of Holthausen 1888 Part I
entities.description: A complete 160-canvas color page-image surrogate of the Bayerische Staatsbibliothek copy of Holthausen's 1888 Part I, shelfmark P.o.angl. 313 r-89, delivered through a public IIIF Presentation v2 manifest.
entities.reproduction_format: 160 color page images exposed through IIIF Presentation API v2; local research bundle contains manifest, inventory, and 160 JPEG derivatives

entities.label: Judith M. Crawford, Vices and Virtues re-edition (1987 thesis)
entities.description: A later direct re-edition from British Library Stowe MS 34, with a substantial study of manuscript, language, sources, genre, date, and transmission. The repository citation assigns 1987; the internal submission title page says November 1986.

date_statements.label: repository publication/citation
date_statements.notes: The thesis title page itself records submission in November 1986.

bibliographic.citation: Judith M. Crawford, 'Vices and Virtues': Re-edited from British Library MS Stowe 34 (PhD thesis, University of Sheffield, 1987; submitted November 1986).
bibliographic.title: 'Vices and Virtues': Re-edited from British Library MS Stowe 34

notes: The edited text occupies thesis pp. 1-75, corresponding to PDF pp. 69-143 and witness ff. 1r-48r.
Crawford is a collateral modern edition, not an ancestor of the 1997 U-M encoding.

entities.label: White Rose complete PDF surrogate of Crawford's thesis
entities.description: The complete 291-page White Rose PDF delivery surrogate of Crawford's thesis. It reproduces the whole thesis expression and, at PDF pp. 6-7, embeds two selected manuscript photographs: Stowe MS 34 f. 18v and f. 43v. Complete PDF coverage is therefore only partial facsimile coverage of the manuscript witness.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete downloadable PDF thesis surrogate containing two black-and-white manuscript figure pages

entities.label: Ferdinand Holthausen, Vices and Virtues, Part II: Notes and Glossary (1921)
entities.description: The separate EETS Original Series 159 supporting apparatus keyed to Part I. It contains notes, glossary entries, lemmata, quotations, and source discussion but no continuous edition of the Middle English work. The schema has no supporting-study type, so catalog_description is used provisionally.

bibliographic.citation: Ferdinand Holthausen, Vices and Virtues, Part II: Notes and Glossary, EETS Original Series 159 (London: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society, 1921 [for 1920]).
bibliographic.title: Vices and Virtues, Part II: Notes and Glossary
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 159
bibliographic.publisher: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: Part II is supporting scholarship and apparatus. It is not a second witness and not an ancestor of the U-M Part I text.
catalog_description is a provisional schema workaround for a separately delivered supporting apparatus volume with no continuous target text.

entities.label: Cate Gunn, Vices and Virtues: A Reassessment of Manuscript Stowe 34 (2012)
entities.description: A peer-reviewed work-specific chapter reassessing manuscript date, composition, region, vice taxonomy, homiletic affinities, readership, pastoral reform, and spiritual guidance. Only the public abstract and metadata were consulted in this review.

bibliographic.citation: Cate Gunn, 'Vices and Virtues: A Reassessment of Manuscript Stowe 34,' in Sin in Medieval and Early Modern Culture (2012), 65-84.
bibliographic.title: Vices and Virtues: A Reassessment of Manuscript Stowe 34

object.description: Surviving acephalous Vices and Virtues text

scope.notes: The 1888 title uses the then-current shelfmark Stowe MS 240; this is the same physical witness now cataloged as Stowe MS 34.

assertion.notes: EETS's exact backmatter wording calls OS 89 'Vices and Virtues, from the unique MS., ab. 1200 a.d., ed. Dr. F. Holthausen'; Part II's title says it was edited 'from the Stowe MS. 240'; and its notes and glossary repeatedly identify MS readings distinct from the printed text. Crawford's later direct comparison calls the edition sound and thorough with few errors. This supports a continuous transcribes edge but does not document Holthausen's precise autopsy or working-copy method.

access.access_method: Read the pinned XML in the CME git repository or through this repository's submodule path.
Open the U-M item page and browse the sixty-section HTML text.

No public full scan of the exact 1967 issue was verified. The U-M source header identifies its copy as call number 820.6 E13 no.89 1967; consult U-M's current catalog or reference service for physical-copy availability.
access.format: Physical printed reprint; no verified public digital facsimile
notes: The complete public BSB facsimile is the original 1888 issue, not the 1967 carrier.

access.access_method: Browse the complete BSB viewer or use the exact public IIIF Presentation v2 manifest. A complete 160-image research bundle is cached locally.

local_copies.label: Complete BSB IIIF research bundle at effective 1600-pixel image size

work_portion.label: Vices and Virtues Part I text and translation
locators: complete edition printed pp. 2-151, IIIF canvases 10-159
facing modern English even pp. 2-150, canvases 10,12,...,158
notes: The bundle contains the whole 160-canvas source object, not a crop to target pages.

access.format: IIIF page images and local ZIP research bundle

access.access_method: Use the current BL Archives and Manuscripts catalog record and cite current shelfmark Stowe MS 34.

No complete public facsimile was found in the current catalog, official digitised-manuscript list, or tested public endpoint patterns. Recheck the catalog and ask BL reference services before concluding that no internal or unindexed photography exists.
notes: Crawford's thesis supplies only two manuscript photographs, f. 18v and f. 43v.

access.access_method: Register for or renew a British Library Reader Pass, submit the collection-item request form using Stowe MS 34, and consult the manuscript in London. The catalog says a letter of introduction is required.

Submit the BL imaging order form and request a quote for full-color photography of the complete surviving object: every folio recto and verso plus boards, binding, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, edges, detached matter, and any housing or associated matter that can safely be photographed. Ask BL to confirm the current leaf inventory and do not restrict the order to ff. 1r-48r.
access.contact: permissions@bl.uk for reuse permission; licensing@bl.uk is also named on the digitisation service page
access.format: Rare-item photography; request color TIFF explicitly because original manuscripts cannot be scanned
notes: The 2025/26 price card lists photographic TIFF at GBP 27.70 for the first image and GBP 7.25 for each subsequent image. Ninety-six to ninety-eight folio-side images alone would nominally be GBP 716.45-730.95 before boards, flyleaves, blanks, housing, handling, or other charges; obtain a formal quote.
The work association remains ff. 1r-48r even though the requested reproduction should cover the entire surviving manuscript object.

access.access_method: Ask BL Reference Services whether existing unindexed photography, legacy microfilm, conservation imaging, or other surrogates cover Stowe MS 34 and request confirmation of the complete folio/leaf inventory before ordering.
Open the White Rose repository landing page for the thesis's bibliographic record, abstract, citation, and direct-file link.
Download the complete thesis PDF directly. A complete research copy is cached locally; the work's edited text is at PDF pp. 69-143 and the two selected manuscript figures are at PDF pp. 6-7.

local_copies.label: Complete White Rose PDF surrogate of Crawford's thesis

work_portion.label: Crawford's direct manuscript re-edition of Vices and Virtues
notes: The complete thesis PDF is retained, not a crop to the edition pages.
Separate facsimile_of scope records the two embedded witness photographs at PDF pp. 6-7 as partial manuscript coverage.

access.format: Complete PDF thesis surrogate with two embedded manuscript figures

access.access_method: Use the Internet Archive item page or exact direct PDF download. The complete PDF is cached locally.

local_copies.label: Complete Holthausen 1921 Part II PDF
notes: The complete source is supporting notes and glossary. It contains quoted lemmata and readings but no continuous target-work text, so no work_portion is asserted.

access.access_method: Download the exact official EETS PDF. A complete copy is cached locally.

local_copies.label: Complete EETS 2026 publications list
notes: The complete forty-page catalog is cached. Part I is listed on PDF p. 10 and Part II on PDF p. 14, but the catalog contains metadata rather than the target work text.

access.access_method: Purchase the current paperback/backlist product from an OUP-linked bookseller or locate it through the OUP EETS catalog. The exact printing ancestry should be inspected after receipt.
access.format: Paperback; possibly print on demand, exact manufacture unverified
notes: The official 2026 list gives GBP 20.00 / USD 34.95. Retailer pricing and availability may differ.

access.access_method: Download the official U.S. Copyright Office PDF. A complete copy is cached locally.

local_copies.label: Complete U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, April 2026
notes: This is general legal guidance and contains no Vices and Virtues text.

rights.component: The anonymous medieval Middle English and embedded Latin textual work
rights.basis: The work was composed and copied around 1200; no modern intellectual contribution is included in this component.

rights.component: The medieval text, scribal writing, corrections, rubrication, and other historical manuscript content embodied in Stowe MS 34
rights.basis: The manuscript content was created in the early thirteenth century. Physical ownership, consultation rules, and rights or terms in modern photography remain separate layers.
notes: This record does not authorize access to the physical object or reuse of British Library photographs; those are covered separately.

rights.component: Holthausen's 1888 edited text, translation, introduction, and published edition as a work
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1888, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff described in U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A.

rights.component: The unchanged 1888 verbal and editorial content retained in the 1967 reprint
rights.basis: The reprint statement and matching pagination identify retained content from the public-domain 1888 edition; republication does not create a new U.S. copyright term in unchanged public-domain material.

rights.component: Any new 1967 title-page wording, typography, layout, production matter, or other independently created additions
rights.basis: The 1967 carrier has distinct preliminaries and imprint. No item-specific rights statement or complete comparison proving zero new protectable matter was found.

rights.component: The current U-M digital item and encoding in the United States
rights.basis: The current U-M item presents the materials as public domain and displays the RightsStatements.org No Copyright-United States designation.
notes: NoC-US is territorially limited and does not itself grant copyright permission where other rights or contractual restrictions apply.
The pinned 1997 XML separately preserves a more restrictive redistribution notice.

rights.component: Redistribution terms embedded in the 1997 XML header
rights.basis: The header allows individuals to copy for personal use, research, and teaching with attribution and permits linking, including by for-profit organizations, but expressly forbids redistribution by commercial or nonprofit content providers.
notes: This provider-term layer is recorded separately from U-M's current NoC-US display and from copyright in the underlying medieval and 1888 text.

rights.component: The checked-in XML artifact, including contributor-owned repository additions, inherited U-M transcription and markup, and the copied provider statement
rights.jurisdiction: Repository licence assertion; inherited-source scope unresolved
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE is the Unlicense and broadly permits use and dedicates copyright interests in 'software.' It does not expressly identify the imported U-M XML corpus, data, transcription, encoding, or markup as covered software, and no path-specific notice was found. The repository assertion is therefore recorded for contributor-owned software and repository contributions without treating it as blanket permission for inherited material or as overriding the XML's embedded 1997 U-M redistribution terms.
notes: The Unlicense assertion is attributed to CME for contributor-owned software and repository contributions; this record does not infer that CME contributors own or can relicense inherited U-M XML, data, transcription, encoding, or markup.
The CME LICENSE does not expressly reconcile or supersede either U-M's current No Copyright-United States display or the more restrictive redistribution terms embedded in the 1997 XML.

rights.component: The BSB/Google digital page images and delivered derivatives
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest assigns RightsStatements.org No Copyright-Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0. BSB's FAQ permits reuse under its stated noncommercial conditions and describes limited commercial contexts in which the scan images are not the predominant or sole content.
notes: Required attribution described by BSB includes the holding library, shelfmark, page or folio, URN, and the phrase digitized by Google.
The public-domain status of the 1888 edition is not treated as canceling provider conditions attached to the scan service.

rights.component: Crawford's thesis prose, edition, apparatus, organization, and presentation
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable territories
rights.basis: White Rose states that where no Creative Commons license is displayed, copyright remains with the author or other owner. No CC license appears on this thesis record.
notes: Public download access is not a general redistribution or republication license. Public-domain manuscript text must be separated from Crawford's modern editorial expression.

rights.component: The complete White Rose thesis PDF delivery surrogate, including the two reproduced manuscript photographs at PDF pp. 6-7
rights.basis: The thesis download carries no CC license, Crawford's modern thesis remains copyrighted, and the embedded manuscript figures may involve British Library or photographer rights distinct from the public-domain manuscript text.

rights.component: Holthausen's 1921 notes and glossary as a published work
rights.basis: The volume was published in 1921, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff described in U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A.

rights.component: The Internet Archive-delivered scan and derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1921 edition is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific open license for the delivered scan was verified.

rights.component: The current paperback/POD carrier, new production matter, and any independently protected additions
rights.basis: EETS states that normal copyright laws cover Society volumes and directs large-scale reproduction enquiries to its Executive Secretary. The exact current printing ancestry and new matter were not inspected.
notes: This record does not retract the United States public-domain status of unchanged 1888 content; it isolates unknown/current carrier contributions and provider statements.

rights.component: Any manuscript photographs supplied or newly made by the British Library
rights.jurisdiction: British Library imaging terms and applicable territories
rights.basis: BL imaging terms distinguish supply from reuse and require written permission for publication or other reuse; no item-specific image set or license has yet been supplied.
notes: Request written terms that separately address research use, repository redistribution, commercial and nonprofit use, print-on-demand use, attribution, modification, and sublicensing.
The underlying medieval work remains public domain; this record addresses only supplied image files and provider terms.

rights.component: The 2026 EETS publications-list text, arrangement, typography, and PDF delivery file
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable territories
rights.basis: The current society catalog is a modern publication and displays no item-specific open license. Public download is not treated as permission to republish the catalog PDF.

rights.component: The modern MED bibliography record, database arrangement, links, and interface presentation
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable territories
rights.basis: The official record is publicly viewable but no item-specific public-domain dedication or open reuse license was verified for the modern database record and presentation.
notes: This does not affect public-domain status of the medieval work or bare historical facts such as shelfmarks and dates.

rights.component: The official Circular 15A text and PDF delivery file
rights.basis: The circular is an official U.S. Copyright Office publication and is publicly downloadable, but this manifest did not verify an item-specific rights statement or separately document the statutory federal-government-work rule for the delivery file.

rights.component: Gunn's 2012 chapter, abstract, and publisher presentation
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable territories
rights.basis: The modern Cambridge University Press chapter is paywalled and no open license appears on the inspected public abstract page.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Stowe MS 34 preserves one continuous acephalous Middle English prose text with embedded Latin.
editorial_practices.representation: Holthausen prints a modern English translation on even pp. 2-150 facing the edited Middle English transcription on odd pp. 3-151.
editorial_practices.description: Text and translation are separate edition components with alternating pagination; lineage scopes must state which component is encoded or reused.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The manuscript contains additions, deletions, corrections, underdotting, ambiguous letters, rubrication, and two principal scribal hands.
editorial_practices.representation: Holthausen supplies corrected readings and notes; the U-M header says corrections are Holthausen's unless otherwise noted and describes its assumptions for scribal additions/deletions and underdotting.
editorial_practices.description: The printed text is an edited transcription, not a photographic or fully diplomatic representation of the witness.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English odd pp. 3-151 and associated notes

editorial_practices.source_feature: The 1888 issue has a London N. Trübner title page and original publication matter.
editorial_practices.representation: The 1967 issue retains the Part I body pagination while replacing or adding title/preliminary pages, a London/New York/Toronto OUP imprint, and an explicit first-published/reprinted statement.
editorial_practices.description: The reprint is textually linked to 1888 but remains a distinct physical/editorial carrier.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The 1967 Part I presents modern English translation on even pp. 2-150 and Middle English edited text on odd pp. 3-151.
editorial_practices.representation: U-M encodes three title pages and only the Middle English odd pages, omitting the facing translation while retaining Text and Translation in the bibliographic title.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item is complete for its selected Middle English edition text but incomplete for the source carrier's facing translation and broader preliminary matter.
editorial_practices.scope: U-M FRONT title pages and BODY odd printed pp. 3-151

editorial_practices.source_feature: The witness's exact intrinsic hierarchy cannot be verified without full images, and rubric/hand distinctions require manuscript-level comparison.
editorial_practices.representation: The encoding divides the BODY into sixty DIV1 sections with HEAD elements and displays them as sixty contents entries.
editorial_practices.description: These divisions are exposed as editorial/encoding navigation, not asserted as sixty authorial works or canonical medieval chapters.
editorial_practices.scope: BODY DIV1[1]-DIV1[60] and current U-M contents list

editorial_practices.source_feature: Holthausen's print distinguishes corrected readings, supplied letters, manuscript additions/deletions, notes, and manuscript-page references.
editorial_practices.representation: U-M maps these features to 128 CORR, 182 ADD, 101 DEL, 224 NOTE1, and 95 manuscript-page MILESTONE elements. The header says parentheses around single letters were replaced by superscript and records uncertainty conventions.
editorial_practices.description: Encoding tags preserve editorial interpretation and cannot be treated as direct manuscript glyph shapes without comparing the source edition and witness.
editorial_practices.scope: The complete encoded Middle English BODY

editorial_practices.source_feature: Stowe MS 34 contains multiple scribal, corrective, rubricating, and later hands and an acephalous text.
editorial_practices.representation: Crawford re-edits directly from the manuscript, distinguishes hands, uses square brackets for editorial interventions, and supports emendations through notes and apparatus while consulting Holthausen.
editorial_practices.description: Crawford offers a later independent manuscript-based control source rather than a derivative of the U-M encoding.
editorial_practices.scope: Thesis edited text pp. 1-75/PDF pp. 69-143 and manuscript study

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/Vices+V1.xml, pinned at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: complete file; HEADER, FRONT title pages, BODY, and availability statement
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for the artifact identity and hash, U-M 1997 item, keyboarding and markup history, exact 1967 source call number, 1888/reprinted-1967 statement, selected title pages, odd-page Middle English BODY, legacy provider terms, and encoded editorial conventions.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c (The Unlicense).
evidence.locator: complete 1,211-byte file; git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657
evidence.summary: The file says, 'This is free and unencumbered software released into the public domain,' permits anyone to copy, modify, publish, use, compile, sell, or distribute 'this software,' and has the authors dedicate copyright interests in 'this software.' Its operative language repeatedly scopes the assertion to software and does not expressly identify inherited U-M XML, data, transcription, encoding, or markup or override the XML's embedded 1997 terms.

evidence.citation: XPath structural and language audit of CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/Vices+V1.xml.
evidence.locator: BODY counts and ancestry audit for DIV1, P, NOTE1, PB, MILESTONE, CORR, ADD, DEL, and FOREIGN
evidence.summary: Finds 60 DIV1, 77 P, 224 NOTE1, 75 odd-page PB, 95 manuscript-page MILESTONE, 128 CORR, 182 ADD, 101 DEL, and 299 FOREIGN. Of 295 LAT elements, 280 are in BODY prose outside notes and seven in headings; all four GR tokens occur only inside NOTE1/SIC editorial readings.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Vices and Virtues, CME item VicesV1.
evidence.locator: item information, contents, source statement, stable link, and rights display
evidence.summary: Official current evidence for the item title, Ferdinand Holthausen attribution, source description, sixty contents sections, public digital text, and current public-domain/No Copyright-United States representation.

evidence.citation: Three 1967 title and preliminary pages transcribed in the U-M VicesV1 FRONT.
evidence.locator: TEXT/FRONT title-page structures before BODY
evidence.summary: The pages identify the London/New York/Toronto OUP carrier, say First Published 1888 and Reprinted 1967, retain EETS Original Series 89, name Vivian Ridler at the University Press Oxford, and distinguish the reprint's preliminary matter from the 1888 title page.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek, digital object bsb11665018, Ferdinand Holthausen, Vices and Virtues, Part I (1888), shelfmark P.o.angl. 313 r-89.
evidence.locator: complete 160-canvas IIIF object and manifest metadata
evidence.summary: Official complete surrogate and metadata evidence for the 1888 edition identity, title, creator, extent, BSB shelfmark and identifiers, public IIIF manifest, NoC-NC rights statement, and complete page sequence.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the complete BSB IIIF facsimile of Holthausen 1888 Part I.
evidence.locator: canvas 7 original title page; canvas 8 colophon/OS 89; canvas 9 half-title; canvases 10-159 printed pp. 2-151; canvas 160 trailing blank
evidence.summary: Confirms the original London N. Trübner 1888 title page's statement that the edition was made from Stowe MS 240, the alternating facing translation on even pages and Middle English text on odd pages, retained page sequence, beginning and end of the edited work, and discrepancy with the current EETS list's 1887 date.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek, Digitale Sammlungen FAQ and IIIF rights metadata for bsb11665018.
evidence.locator: reuse, commercial-use, attribution, and rights sections; manifest license and attribution fields
evidence.summary: Official provider evidence for No Copyright-Non-Commercial Use Only, BSB attribution, Google digitization credit, required holding/shelfmark/page/URN citation, noncommercial reuse, and BSB's narrower described commercial-use context when scan images are not the predominant or sole content.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Stowe MS 34, record 040-001952812.
evidence.locator: title, contents, date, material, extent, hand change, binding, provenance, Digitised Content, and access fields
evidence.summary: Current authoritative record for Stowe MS 34 and former Stowe MS 240: unique-looking acephalous vices/virtues text, early-thirteenth-century vellum manuscript, 49-folio extent, change of hand at f. 39, crimson morocco binding, Fleetwood and Astle provenance, blank Digitised Content, London consultation, and collection-item request route.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Stowe MS 240 within Stowe MS 234-240, records 040-001953036 and 038-001953029.
evidence.locator: Stowe MS 240 file and parent-series descriptions
evidence.summary: Current official evidence that Stowe MS 240 is an unrelated nineteenth-century paper transcript volume of Hanover state papers, Bothmar correspondence, and Jacobite letters, not the medieval Vices and Virtues manuscript formerly bearing that number.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitised Manuscripts list, updated 10 October 2025.
evidence.locator: Stowe shelfmark sequence, which moves from Stowe MS 17 to Stowe MS 42 without Stowe MS 34
evidence.summary: Current official list evidence that no public Stowe MS 34 digitised-manuscript entry is listed; this negative result is date-bounded and does not prove the manuscript has never been photographed.

evidence.citation: Dated public-facsimile audit for British Library Stowe MS 34.
evidence.locator: BL catalog Digitised Content field, official digitised list, web searches, and tested plausible IIIF endpoint patterns
evidence.summary: No complete public manuscript facsimile or working IIIF manifest was found as of 2026-07-15. A plausible BL endpoint returned 404 and a legacy Digirati pattern returned 403 without a discoverable item. The result supports no_public_copy_found, not a claim that digitization never occurred.

evidence.citation: British Library collection-item request and Reference Services routes for archives and manuscripts.
evidence.locator: request form, reference enquiry, and Stowe MS 34 catalog access instructions
evidence.summary: Official access evidence that registered or renewed readers can request Stowe MS 34, all manuscripts must be consulted in London, a letter of introduction is required for this item, and reference services can answer availability and surrogate questions.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.locator: order route, formats, color request, and licensing contact
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the online imaging order form, requestable digitisation/photography, color and file-format choices, and the need to address reuse and licensing separately.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.locator: rare-item handling, original-manuscript photography, permissions, delivery, and reuse terms
evidence.summary: Official terms evidence that original manuscripts cannot be scanned, rare items may require photography, color must be specified, supply does not itself grant all reuse rights, and written permission should be sought through permissions@bl.uk.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services price list 2025/26.
evidence.locator: photographic TIFF first-image and subsequent-image prices
evidence.summary: Official price evidence listing photographic TIFF at GBP 27.70 for the first image and GBP 7.25 for each subsequent image; this supports only a nominal folio-side estimate before object-specific handling, housing, binding, flyleaf, blank, edge, or other charges.

evidence.citation: Judith M. Crawford, 'Vices and Virtues': Re-edited from British Library MS Stowe 34 (PhD thesis, University of Sheffield, 1987; submitted November 1986).
evidence.locator: complete 291-page PDF; manuscript and language study; edition thesis pp. 1-75/PDF pp. 69-143; figures PDF pp. 6-7
evidence.summary: Direct manuscript-based scholarship and edition supporting the unique-witness relation, ff. 1r-48r locus, 48 surviving leaves and lost first quire, scribes and corrections, approximate date and region, genre cautions, anonymous authorship, Holthausen consultation, and two selected manuscript photographs.

evidence.citation: Visual and structural inspection of Crawford's complete thesis PDF.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 6-7 manuscript figures; PDF pp. 69-143 edited text; internal November 1986 title page
evidence.summary: Confirms that the PDF is complete as a thesis, that manuscript imagery is limited to f. 18v and f. 43v, that the edited text maps to thesis pp. 1-75/PDF pp. 69-143, and that internal submission and repository citation dates differ by one year.

evidence.citation: White Rose eTheses Online, Copyright guidance.
evidence.locator: copyright ownership and Creative Commons guidance; thesis record license display
evidence.summary: Provider evidence that absent an indicated Creative Commons license, thesis copyright remains with the author or other owner. The Crawford record displays no CC license, so public download is not treated as a general republication license.

evidence.citation: Cate Gunn, 'Vices and Virtues: A Reassessment of Manuscript Stowe 34,' in Sin in Medieval and Early Modern Culture (2012), 65-84.
evidence.locator: public abstract and bibliographic metadata
evidence.summary: Peer-reviewed public summary supporting the unique witness, first-quarter-thirteenth-century manuscript date, probable pre-1200 composition, South-East England and possible Essex context, Cassianic/noncardinal treatment of sins, homily-collection affinities, spiritual guidance, pastoral reform, and broad readership.
notes: The full chapter was not publicly retrieved; the manifest uses only claims exposed in the public abstract and metadata.

evidence.citation: Middle English Compendium, bibliography BIB3516, Dialogue on Vices and Virtues.
evidence.locator: title, citation stencil, manuscript, former shelfmark, LAEME items 64/65/302/303, edition history, and localization
evidence.summary: Official repertory evidence linking Stowe MS 34 formerly 240, the a1225(c1200) citation stencil, Holthausen OS 89 and 159 with 1967 reprint history, and LAEME localization of principal hands, titles, and main corrector to south-western Essex.

evidence.citation: Ferdinand Holthausen, Vices and Virtues, Part II: Notes and Glossary, EETS Original Series 159 (1921), Internet Archive item vicesvirtuesbein02holt.
evidence.locator: complete 138-page PDF; title page and printed pp. 153-268
evidence.summary: Complete-surrogate evidence for the 1921 Part II identity, EETS OS 159, Humphrey Milford/OUP imprint, notes-and-glossary scope, Part I citation structure, and absence of a continuous target-work text.

evidence.citation: Early English Text Society, List of All Publications 2026.
evidence.locator: complete forty-page PDF; Part I on PDF p. 10 and Part II on PDF p. 14; backlist and copyright guidance
evidence.summary: Official current evidence for OS 89 ISBN 9780197222607, paperback price GBP 20/USD 34.95, OS 159 ISBN 9780197221594 and same price, general backlist/POD availability, OUP catalog route, and EETS copyright/reproduction guidance. Its OS 89 historical year 1887 conflicts with the inspected 1888 title page.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright, April 2026.
evidence.locator: complete four-page official circular, April 2026
evidence.summary: Official legal guidance used only for United States term calculations and the public-domain status of the 1888 and 1921 publications; it does not decide provider terms, scan rights, foreign jurisdictions, or modern additions.

open_questions.question: Does the British Library hold unindexed photography, conservation imaging, legacy microfilm, or another complete surrogate of Stowe MS 34, and what research and reuse terms would apply?
next_steps: Ask BL Reference Services about every existing surrogate under both current Stowe MS 34 and former Stowe MS 240 identifiers.

If no suitable surrogate exists, request a quote for full-color photography of every surviving folio recto and verso plus boards, binding, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, edges, detached matter, and housing that can safely be imaged.
Ask BL to confirm the current complete inventory and do not limit the reproduction order to the work's ff. 1r-48r locus.
Obtain written terms for research, repository redistribution, commercial and nonprofit publication, print-on-demand, attribution, modification, and sublicensing before publishing any supplied image.

open_questions.question: Why does the current BL record describe 49 folios while Crawford describes 48 surviving leaves in six gatherings of eight, and exactly where does the principal hand change occur?
next_steps: Ask BL for the current foliation, collation, blank/flyleaf treatment, and item-level inventory.
Compare a complete facsimile against Crawford's collation and the catalog description.
Check whether BL's f. 39 change and Crawford's f. 38r takeover/f. 37v filling describe the same transition under different counting conventions.

open_questions.question: How should three unresolved rights layers be reconciled: U-M's current No Copyright-United States display, the restrictive redistribution statement embedded in the 1997 XML, and CME's root Unlicense whose operative grant repeatedly refers to software? In particular, does U-M consider the 1997 statement superseded, and did CME intend or have authority for the Unlicense to cover the inherited U-M XML, data, transcription, encoding, or markup?
next_steps: Request written clarification from U-M Library that separately addresses copyright, provider terms, commercial and nonprofit redistribution, and reuse of encoding/markup.

Ask the CME maintainers whether the root Unlicense intentionally covers imported corpus XML, data, transcription, encoding, and markup, and what authority supports that scope rather than only contributor-owned software and repository additions.
Trace the artifact's import and modification history to distinguish inherited U-M material from any contributor-owned CME additions.
Until both providers clarify the scope, retain all three assertions without selecting whichever is most permissive or treating the CME LICENSE as an override.
Separate public-domain medieval and 1888 text from modern encoding structure in any reuse analysis.

open_questions.question: Is the current ISBN 9780197222607 paperback a facsimile of the 1888 issue, the 1967 reprint, or another reset or print-on-demand master?
next_steps: Ask EETS or OUP which source master and preliminary matter the current product reproduces.
Physically inspect a current copy's title pages, copyright page, pagination, ISBN, and typographic identity.
Add a version_of or facsimile relation only after direct evidence identifies its ancestry.

open_questions.question: What is the current access and circulation status of the exact U-M source copy at call number 820.6 E13 no.89 1967, and does any public or requestable full facsimile of that carrier exist?
next_steps: Verify the call number in U-M's current library catalog and ask reference staff about onsite, lending, or reproduction access.
Request images of all preliminary pages and representative body pages if needed to verify the 1967 carrier against 1888.
Do not substitute the BSB 1888 scan or current backlist product for the exact 1967 source issue.
